CAR Format

CAR Format

Overview

CAR (Content Addressable aRchive) v1 is a format for storing and transmitting IPLD blocks. It's used for repository export/import and sync operations.

CAR Structure

┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ CAR Header                          │
│ - Version (1)                       │
│ - Root CID                          │
│ - Block count                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
        ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Block 1                             │
│ - CID (varint length + bytes)       │
│ - Data (varint length + bytes)      │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
        ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Block 2                             │
│ - CID                               │
│ - Data                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
        ↓
        ... more blocks ...

CAR Class

@interface CAR : NSObject

// Writing
+ (nullable NSData *)createCAR:(NSArray *)blocks
                      rootCID:(NSString *)rootCid
                        error:(NSError **)error;

// Reading
+ (nullable NSDictionary *)parseCAR:(NSData *)carData error:(NSError **)error;

// Streaming
+ (nullable CAR *)streamingCAR:(NSString *)filePath error:(NSError **)error;

@end

Creating CAR Files

// 1. Collect blocks
NSMutableArray *blocks = [NSMutableArray array];

// Add MST root block
[blocks addObject:@{
    @"cid": rootCid,
    @"data": rootBlockData
}];

// Add record blocks
for (NSString *recordCid in recordCids) {
    [blocks addObject:@{
        @"cid": recordCid,
        @"data": recordBlockData
    }];
}

// 2. Create CAR
NSError *error = nil;
NSData *carData = [CAR createCAR:blocks rootCID:rootCid error:&error];

if (carData) {
    // Write to file or send over network
    [carData writeToFile:@"/tmp/repo.car" atomically:YES];
}

Reading CAR Files

// 1. Read CAR file
NSData *carData = [NSData dataWithContentsOfFile:@"/tmp/repo.car"];

// 2. Parse CAR
NSError *error = nil;
NSDictionary *carContent = [CAR parseCAR:carData error:&error];

if (carContent) {
    NSString *rootCid = carContent[@"root"];
    NSArray *blocks = carContent[@"blocks"];

    // Process blocks
    for (NSDictionary *block in blocks) {
        NSString *cid = block[@"cid"];
        NSData *data = block[@"data"];

        // Store block
        [blobService storeBlock:data forCID:cid];
    }
}

Streaming CAR

For large repositories, stream CAR data:

// 1. Create streaming CAR
NSError *error = nil;
CAR *streamingCar = [CAR streamingCAR:@"/tmp/repo.car" error:&error];

// 2. Read blocks one at a time
NSDictionary *block = nil;
while ((block = [streamingCar nextBlock]) != nil) {
    NSString *cid = block[@"cid"];
    NSData *data = block[@"data"];

    // Process block
    [self processBlock:data forCID:cid];
}

Repository Export

// 1. Get repository contents
NSError *error = nil;
NSData *carData = [repositoryService getRepoContents:userDid
                                               since:nil
                                               error:&error];

// 2. Send CAR to client
response.statusCode = 200;
response.body = carData;
[response setHeaderValue:@"application/vnd.ipld.car" forName:@"Content-Type"];

Repository Import

// 1. Receive CAR data
NSData *carData = request.body;

// 2. Parse CAR
NSError *error = nil;
NSDictionary *carContent = [CAR parseCAR:carData error:&error];

if (!carContent) {
    [XrpcErrorHelper setValidationError:response message:@"Invalid CAR"];
    return;
}

// 3. Apply to repository
BOOL success = [repositoryService updateRepo:userDid
                                      commit:carData
                                       error:&error];

if (!success) {
    [XrpcErrorHelper setInternalServerError:response];
    return;
}

Best Practices

  1. CAR Creation
  2. Include all required blocks
  3. Set correct root CID
  4. Validate block data

  5. Use streaming for large repos

  6. CAR Reading

  7. Validate CAR format
  8. Verify root CID
  9. Check block integrity

  10. Handle missing blocks

  11. Performance

  12. Stream large CAR files
  13. Batch block processing
  14. Use compression if needed
  15. Monitor memory usage
